title: Remove the --registry flag from podman search
body:
Instead of setting the --registry flag to search a single registry,
prefix the registry before the image name in the input, an example is
`podman search registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora` and this will search for
the fedora image in only registry.fedoraproject.org.

Fixes https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/977

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: Does this mean that registry names (in `registries.conf`) will mask image names that start with that registry name?  Or are image names not allowed to contain a `.` before their first `/` while registry names must contain a `.` and cannot contain `/` (or some such to make name overlap impossible)?

Also, by removing the `StringSliceFlag`, the previous:

```console
$ podman search --registry a.example.com --registry b.example.com my-alpine
```

is now the harder-to-alias:

```console
$ podman search a.example.com/my-alpine b.example.com/my-alpine
```

I'm not as concerned about that as I am about [namespace collisions][1], but thought I'd point it out in case it impacted the decision.

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1011#issuecomment-400772459

If an object has a . and a / then it is considered a registry, at least that is what the code is doing.
If it contains only a single / then it is considered an image name
dan/walsh is an image on a registry
dan.walsh/dwalsh is dwalsh on dan.walsh registry
dan.walsh is a nimage on a registry name dan.walsh

dan.walsh/dwalsh                       latest   b038d5051184   4 seconds ago    282MB
localhost/dan/dwalsh                   latest   16b2ade6e8a8   10 seconds ago   282MB
localhost/dan.dwalsh                   latest   6d11d7bcd694   22 seconds ago   282MB

What @rhatdan said. Also removing the --registry flag limits the user to search only one registry at a time if a registry is prefixed in the search term. This matches docker search functionality and that is what we want I believe.

> If an object has a . and a / then it is considered a registry, at least that is what the code is doing.
> If it contains only a single / then it is considered an image name

Hmm, so localhost/dan.dwalsh would be an image name?  "Search for localhost/dan.dwalsh on all registries", not "search for dan.dwalsh on localhost"?  That seems surprising to me, but maybe localhost registries are rare enough that we don't care?
Well I created all of these names using podman build, that is why they have localhost in them.
